scaleCompatibility.rationale: The most consonant notes in B major, omitting the 4th (E) and 7th (A#) which create tension. Creates a universally pleasing major sound over any B major harmony
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Perfect for improvisation with guaranteed consonance. Works over any B major chord progression (I-IV-V). Ideal for creating memorable, singable melodies and guitar solos
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for improvisation with guaranteed consonance. Works over any B major chord progression (I-IV-V). Ideal for creating memorable, singable melodies and guitar solos

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Primary major scale - complete B major diatonic collection
scaleCompatibility.rationale: The fundamental major scale for the key of B. Contains all seven diatonic notes that define B major tonality and harmony
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Use when you want full major scale color and movement. Essential for melodic development and complete harmonic expression in B major. Works over all diatonic B major progressions
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use when you want full major scale color and movement. Essential for melodic development and complete harmonic expression in B major. Works over all diatonic B major progressions

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Major scale with b7 (A# becomes A) - V mode of E major, creates dominant 7th character
scaleCompatibility.rationale: One note difference from major (lowered 7th) creates dominant 7th tonality. The b7 is essential for blues, rock, and dominant chord harmony
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Perfect over B7 chords and dominant vamps. Essential for rock progressions using I7-IV7. Creates that classic rock/blues sound when used over major chord forms
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect over B7 chords and dominant vamps. Essential for rock progressions using I7-IV7. Creates that classic rock/blues sound when used over major chord forms

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Major scale with #4 (E becomes E#/F) - IV mode of F# major, creates dreamy, ethereal quality
scaleCompatibility.rationale: One note difference from major (raised 4th) creates a brighter, more ethereal sound. The #4 adds a characteristic 'floating' quality beloved in jazz and progressive rock
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Use over Bmaj7 or Bmaj7#11 chords. Perfect for I chord modal vamps. Emphasize the E#/F for the characteristic Lydian color
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use over Bmaj7 or Bmaj7#11 chords. Perfect for I chord modal vamps. Emphasize the E#/F for the characteristic Lydian color

scaleCompatibility.relationship: ii minor pentatonic in B major system - modal pentatonic relationship
scaleCompatibility.rationale: Provides a different minor color while staying in the B major/G# minor universe. Creates interesting tension and resolution possibilities
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Use over ii chords (C#m) in B major. Great for modal interchange and adding variety to B major improvisations
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use over ii chords (C#m) in B major. Great for modal interchange and adding variety to B major improvisations

scaleCompatibility.relationship: IV chord scale - subdominant relationship in B major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: E major is the IV of B major; shares 6 of 7 notes (only A#â†’A). Creates the characteristic IV chord color essential to major key harmony
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Perfect for emphasizing IV chord function. Use over I-IV-V progressions. The natural A instead of A# creates authentic subdominant color
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for emphasizing IV chord function. Use over I-IV-V progressions. The natural A instead of A# creates authentic subdominant color

scaleCompatibility.relationship: vi scale - relative minor, shares all notes with B major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: G# minor is the relative minor of B major; they share all seven notes. Provides the minor color within the B major key system
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Use when moving to vi chords in B major. Perfect for creating emotional contrast. Works for full minor sections within primarily major songs
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use when moving to vi chords in B major. Perfect for creating emotional contrast. Works for full minor sections within primarily major songs

scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Use over ii chords (C#m) in B major. Great for modal interchange and adding variety to B major improvisations
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use over ii chords (C#m) in B major. Great for modal interchange and adding variety to B major improvisations

scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for emphasizing IV chord function. Use over I-IV-V progressions. The natural A instead of A# creates authentic subdominant color
